search_name: Head of Business Development, US Specialty

candidates.flash_summary: PhD biochemist turned BD professional with end-to-end deal execution across nephrology, neurology, and bone health at Alexion. Science-first approach with expanding China biotech network.
key_strengths: PhD biochem with postdoc at NIH and Harvard; science-first BD approach
End-to-end deal execution from search through CEO presentation at Alexion
Direct therapeutic area alignment: nephrology, neurology, bone health

scope_match.candidate_actual: Associate Director level at Alexion (large pharma)
scope_match.role_requirement: Head of BD role targeting $200M to $500M innovative revenue growth

scope_match.candidate_actual: Individual contributor with cross-functional team leadership
scope_match.role_requirement: Senior individual contributor with German/Swiss team support

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based with expanding China biotech focus
scope_match.role_requirement: US/China asset sourcing for European commercialization

scope_match.candidate_actual: Nephrology, transplant, neurology, bone health, endocrinology
Preclinical through Phase II complete experience

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Rama presents exceptional therapeutic area alignment with direct experience in STADA's core focus areas (nephrology, neurology, bone health) and proven end-to-end BD capability from search through transaction execution. Her expanding China biotech network directly supports STADA's geographic sourcing strategy. The critical question is cultural fit — her emphasis on empowered, collaborative team dynamics may face tension with STADA's remote structure and German-based support model.

key_criteria.name: Buying-side business development experience
key_criteria.evidence: Rama leads comprehensive buy-side BD activities at Alexion including search and evaluation, cross-functional due diligence, business case development, and CEO-level presentations. She has completed full acquisitions, collaboration agreements with option to acquire, and asset license deals spanning preclinical through Phase II-complete stage assets.

key_criteria.name: End-to-end deal execution capability
key_criteria.evidence: She manages complete deal cycles from search through transaction execution, drafting non-binding term sheets, leading negotiations, and completing deal cycles. Demonstrated independent capability by identifying a newly characterized disease opportunity, educating leadership, conducting directional forecasts herself, and presenting to CEO without internal research or clinical champions.

key_criteria.name: US biotech network and relationships
key_criteria.evidence: Boston-based with established networks in the VC community and contacts in China's business development community. Has worked at Alexion and Vertex in the heart of Boston's biotech ecosystem. Increasing focus on Chinese companies due to heating biotech activity in Asia Pacific region.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Alexion Pharmaceuticals and broader Boston ecosystem

key_criteria.name: Scientific depth and technical fluency
key_criteria.evidence: PhD in biochemistry and molecular biology, postdoctoral research at NIH and Harvard Medical School, plus MBA from MIT. Demonstrates modality-agnostic expertise across small molecules, antibodies, gene therapy, cell therapy, and oligonucleotides. Leads BD opportunities with science-first approach and has educated leadership on newly characterized diseases.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at NIH, Harvard Medical School, and Alexion Pharmaceuticals

key_criteria.name: Entrepreneurial and independent operator
key_criteria.evidence: Confirmed comfort with independent operation, stating 'Building therapeutic area strategy to identifying assets, filtering noise and really championing the deal is what I've been doing is what I love'. Led deals entirely from scratch without internal champions, comfortable with extensive travel, and operates autonomously while leading cross-functional teams.

compensation.flexibility: Deferred compensation discussion until fit is established — prioritizes cultural and strategic alignment first
compensation.budget_range: Base salary anticipated to start with 2, maybe 3 (unclear if $200K-$300K range intended)

why_interested.headline: Direct role alignment
why_interested.detail: Role description matches her current end-to-end BD work including therapeutic areas (nephrology, neurology, bone health), CEO-level engagement, and deal execution.

why_interested.headline: Seeking empowered environment
why_interested.detail: Wants an environment where she feels heard and empowered to make impact, with space for healthy debates and evidence-based decision-making rather than having ideas dismissed.

potential_concerns.concern: Raised specific concerns about STADA's strategic clarity, questioning whether it's 'precision therapy' focus or 'just a revenue play' and seeking understanding of long-term leadership stability given PE transition timing
Strong emphasis on collaborative team culture with 'space for healthy debates' may face tension with STADA's remote individual contributor structure without US office presence
No evidence of European market commercialization experience or understanding of European regulatory and reimbursement landscape for asset evaluation

our_take.text: We believe Rama represents a strong technical fit with exceptional therapeutic area alignment and proven end-to-end BD capability. Her PhD background, science-first approach, and track record identifying novel assets in newly characterized diseases align well with STADA's need for scientific fluency and entrepreneurial dealmaking. However, her sophisticated questions about strategic vision and leadership stability signal she will require substantive clarity on STADA's mission and organizational coherence before committing. Worth discussing with the client whether her emphasis on empowered, collaborative culture can be satisfied within STADA's remote structure.
discussion_points: Strategic vision clarity: Is STADA pursuing precision therapy focus or pure revenue growth mandate?
Cultural fit assessment: Can STADA's remote structure with German support provide the empowered, collaborative environment she seeks?
Leadership stability and organizational coherence during PE transition
Her China biotech network depth and specific relationships that could accelerate Asian sourcing strategy
our_take.ai_rationale: I structured this Our Take to balance strong technical fit against cultural fit concerns, emphasizing her direct therapeutic alignment and proven capabilities while highlighting her sophisticated due diligence questions as both positive (strategic thinking) and concerning (potential barriers to acceptance). I recommended ADVANCE based on strong technical credentials but flagged key discussion points the client must address to secure her interest.

edc_data.search_name: Head of Business Development, US Specialty · STADA
edc_data.role_title: Head of Business Development, US Specialty

candidates.flash_summary: PhD biomedical engineer with 3 completed in-licensing deals at AbbVie including $1.2B Aliada neuroscience transaction. Former BCG biopharma strategy consultant with strong scientific credentials.
key_strengths: 3 completed in-licensing deals including $1.2B Aliada neuroscience transaction
PhD biomedical engineering with neuroscience focus from Georgia Tech Emory
Global asset evaluation including significant recent China activity

scope_match.candidate_actual: 3 completed in-licensing deals including $1.2B Aliada transaction; clinical-stage and discovery assets
scope_match.role_requirement: Late-stage assets (post-Phase II and filed compounds)

scope_match.candidate_actual: PhD biomedical engineering with neuroscience focus; 3 years evaluating neuroscience and eye care assets
scope_match.role_requirement: PhD or MD background with technical fluency

scope_match.candidate_actual: Global asset evaluation including significant China activity
scope_match.role_requirement: US and Chinese biotech sourcing focus

scope_match.candidate_actual: Seeking team leadership role and organizational growth
scope_match.role_requirement: Individual contributor operating remotely without US office

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Borden brings proven buy-side execution with three completed deals including a $1.2B transaction, backed by strong scientific credentials. However, two critical gaps emerge: his deals focused on clinical-stage and discovery assets rather than the late-stage compounds STADA specifically targets, and his stated aspiration to "lead a team" conflicts with the entrepreneurial solo operator model required for this remote, individual contributor role.

key_criteria.name: Buying-side business development experience
key_criteria.evidence: Almost three years of dedicated buy-side search and evaluation experience at AbbVie, where he has completed three full in-licensing deals plus participated in final diligence for the Cerebell M&A acquisition. Has due diligenced "hundreds of companies" and confirmed that "all of the deals I've done have been licensing deals" on the in-licensing side.

key_criteria.name: End-to-end deal execution capability
key_criteria.evidence: Completed three full deals at AbbVie including the Aliada transaction ($1.2B for brain shuttle technology) and the Aterex collaboration (multi-TA discovery collaboration with "a couple hundred million upfront"). Also participated in the Cerebell acquisition where he "performed the final leg of diligence through the M&A of the company."

key_criteria.name: US biotech network and relationships
key_criteria.evidence: Travels "at least one to two full weeks a quarter, probably close to two weeks a quarter" attending conferences including JPMorgan and BIO, suggesting active engagement in the US biotech ecosystem. However, depth of senior-level relationships with CEOs, CBOs, or Heads of R&D was not explored in the interview.

key_criteria.name: Scientific depth and technical fluency
key_criteria.evidence: Holds a PhD in biomedical engineering with a neuroscience focus from Georgia Tech Emory. Has almost three years in AbbVie's Search & Evaluation group focused on neuroscience and eye care, having due diligenced "hundreds of companies" and completed neuroscience-focused transactions including the Aliada brain shuttle technology deal.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at AbbVie and Georgia Tech Emory

key_criteria.name: Entrepreneurial and independent operator
key_criteria.evidence: Expressed interest in the STADA opportunity but stated he is "looking for a bigger role" and "hoping to lead a team at some point," noting "sounds like I'd be the only hire" and expressing hope "we could grow to a team of individuals." This suggests orientation toward team-building rather than solo entrepreneurial operation.

compensation.flexibility: Used "probably" and "closer to" suggesting some flexibility around the $500K figure

why_interested.headline: Leadership opportunity
why_interested.detail: Interested because "I could lead the group which is something that I'm interested to do more of" and hopes to "grow to a team of individuals to really shape the business development opportunities."

why_interested.headline: Seeking bigger role
why_interested.detail: Stated he is "looking for a bigger role" after having "done a couple of deals and kind of understand how it goes" at AbbVie.

potential_concerns.concern: No late-stage deal experience - acknowledged "I haven't been involved in late stage" and philosophy that "usually there's a reason why they're late stage and not picked up already" contrasts with STADA's focus on post-Phase II and filed assets
Role structure misalignment - explicitly seeking team leadership opportunities while STADA requires entrepreneurial individual contributor operating remotely without US office infrastructure
US biotech network depth not validated - while attends major conferences, senior-level relationships with CEOs/CBOs not explored

our_take.text: We believe Borden presents strong technical credentials and proven deal execution capability that merit continued evaluation, but two fundamental concerns require direct client discussion. His acknowledged lack of late-stage experience and stated philosophy about late-stage assets conflicts directly with STADA's mandate to source post-Phase II and filed compounds. Additionally, his explicit career goal to "lead a team" and expectation to "grow to a team of individuals" suggests potential misalignment with the entrepreneurial solo operator model STADA requires. While his scientific depth and $1.2B deal track record are compelling, these structural gaps may indicate he views this as a stepping stone rather than a long-term strategic fit.
discussion_points: Comfort level and track record operating independently without organizational support or US office infrastructure
Willingness to focus exclusively on late-stage assets despite philosophical concerns about why they remain available
Long-term interest in individual contributor role versus team leadership aspirations
Depth of senior-level relationships within US biotech ecosystem to represent STADA as European partner of choice
our_take.ai_rationale: Generated Our Take from EDS evidence since no manual notes were provided. Emphasized the two most critical structural concerns (late-stage experience gap and role structure misalignment) that emerged consistently throughout the assessment, while acknowledging his strong technical qualifications and deal track record that make him worth continued evaluation rather than immediate dismissal.

candidates.current_title: Associate Director, Global Business Development & Strategy
candidates.flash_summary: Medicinal chemist from Pfizer/Novartis turned BD professional at Otsuka. Closed $600M Phase I immunology acquisition. Exceptional cultural adaptability across 6 countries.
key_strengths: Closed $600M Phase I mAb acquisition in immunology at Otsuka
Drug discovery background from Pfizer and Novartis across modalities
Cross-cultural navigation across Japan, Europe, and US markets

edc_data.current_title: Associate Director, Global Business Development & Strategy

scope_match.candidate_actual: 2-3 years of active buy-side deal execution
Scientific due diligence and supporting negotiations/commercial evaluation
scope_match.role_requirement: Full end-to-end deal execution capability as independent operator
Nephrology, neurology, oncology, immunology, bone health

scope_match.candidate_actual: Preclinical to commercial, regularly Phase II/III, up to $600M-$1B deals
Medicinal chemistry background, drug discovery at Pfizer/Novartis
scope_match.role_requirement: Strong scientific fluency for technical discussions

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Genovino presents a compelling scientific profile with exceptional therapeutic area alignment and proven deal capability through a $600M Phase I acquisition, but faces a significant experience gap with only 2-3 years of active deal work versus the 5-10 year target. His role has emphasized supporting rather than leading full transaction execution, raising questions about readiness for the autonomous, senior-level dealmaker mandate STADA requires as their sole US representative without local infrastructure support.

key_criteria.name: Buying-side business development experience
key_criteria.evidence: Genovino has 2-3 years of active buy-side deal experience at Otsuka Pharmaceutical, leading scientific due diligence and supporting negotiations for asset acquisitions. He closed a $600M Phase I monoclonal antibody acquisition in the IL-1 pathway for autoimmune diseases and evaluates deals from preclinical to commercial stage with values ranging up to $1B+. However, his deal sheet shows limited closure track record with one major deal and several projects that did not close.

key_criteria.name: End-to-end deal execution capability
key_criteria.evidence: His current role is described as "very much focused on the science" and "supporting negotiations or the commercial evaluation" rather than independently leading full transaction lifecycle. He coordinates with BD, commercial, and transaction teams globally but does not appear to drive deals autonomously from identification through contract execution without dedicated transaction support.

key_criteria.name: US biotech network and relationships
key_criteria.evidence: Genovino has established presence in US biotech ecosystem through regular attendance at major partnering conferences (JPM, BIO) and evaluation of US-based opportunities. He is based in Princeton, New Jersey, a key pharma/biotech hub, and has global deal scope with primary US focus. However, his Associate Director title and supporting role may limit senior-level engagement capabilities.

key_criteria.name: Scientific depth and technical fluency
key_criteria.evidence: Genovino has exceptional scientific credentials with medicinal chemistry background from drug discovery roles at Pfizer and Novartis in cardiometabolic and oncology. His role explicitly focuses on leading scientific due diligence, and he has worked across multiple modalities including small molecules, monoclonal antibodies, gene therapies, and radiopharmaceuticals. His therapeutic area experience spans immunology, CNS, nephrology, and rare diseases.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Pfizer and Novartis, continued at Otsuka

key_criteria.name: Entrepreneurial and independent operator
key_criteria.evidence: Genovino demonstrates entrepreneurial mindset through his self-directed career transition from bench science to BD via MBA, startup consulting experience, and building Otsuka's immunology BD strategy from ground zero. He has successfully navigated cultural complexity and remote collaboration with European and Japanese colleagues, stating he is "not worried about working German company" after Japanese company experience.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Otsuka and previous startup consulting

compensation.expected_base: Step up from Associate Director level
compensation.flexibility: Flexible on structure; interested in standard package (base + bonus + LTI)

why_interested.headline: Career growth opportunity
why_interested.detail: Seeks to develop his career and progress, viewing opportunities like STADA as vehicles to boost his career in many ways.

why_interested.headline: Cultural challenges at Japanese company
why_interested.detail: Faces clear cultural differences including communication issues from language barriers and significantly different risk tolerance between US and Japan colleagues.

potential_concerns.concern: Limited deal execution experience with only 2-3 years of active deal work versus role requirement of 5-10 years - candidate self-identified this as potentially 'borderline for the role'
Current role emphasizes supporting negotiations rather than leading end-to-end transaction execution independently, which may not demonstrate autonomous dealmaker capability required
Limited deal closure track record with one major deal and several non-closed projects may not constitute strong track record for senior-level engagement
Associate Director title may create positioning challenges when engaging at CEO/CBO/Head of R&D level as STADA's sole US representative

our_take.text: We believe Genovino presents a strong scientific foundation and cultural adaptability that would serve STADA well, but his experience level represents a meaningful gap for this senior autonomous role. His medicinal chemistry background, therapeutic area alignment, and successful $600M deal closure demonstrate core capabilities, yet his 2-3 years of active deal experience falls well short of the 5-10 year target. Most critically, his current role emphasizes scientific support rather than independent deal leadership, raising questions about readiness for the full transaction autonomy STADA requires as their sole US representative. His self-awareness about these limitations and integrity in flagging potential gaps is commendable, but he appears to be 3-5 years early in his career trajectory for this mandate.
discussion_points: Assess readiness for autonomous deal leadership without dedicated transaction team support
Validate senior-level engagement capability with biotech CEOs/CBOs given Associate Director positioning
Explore specific examples of independent deal execution beyond the single $600M closure
Understand comfort level operating as sole US presence without local office infrastructure
our_take.ai_rationale: I structured the Our Take to balance Genovino's clear strengths (scientific background, cultural adaptability, therapeutic alignment) against the significant experience gaps he himself identified. I emphasized the central tension between his potential and his current trajectory being 3-5 years early for this role. The HOLD recommendation reflects that while he has foundational capabilities, the experience and autonomy gaps are substantial enough to warrant careful validation before advancing.
